stem: Let \( N \) be a normal subgroup of \( G \) with \( |G| = [[gord]] \) and \( |N| = [[h]] \). What is the order of the quotient group \( G/N \)?
solution: The order of a quotient group is \( |G/N| = |G|/|N| = [[gord]]/[[h]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This restates \( |N| \) instead of dividing \( |G| \) by it.
This restates \( |G| \) instead of computing the quotient's order.
This is off by one from the correct quotient order.
This is off by one from the correct quotient order.

stem: In \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]} \), let \( H = \langle [[d]] \rangle \) be the subgroup generated by [[d]] (recall \( [[n]]=[[d]]\cdot[[m]] \), so \( H \) has order [[m]]). What is the order of the quotient group \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]}/H \)?
solution: Since \( |H|=[[m]] \), the quotient has order \( |\mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]}/H| = [[n]]/[[m]] = [[d]] = [[answer]] \) (in fact \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]}/H \cong \mathbb{Z}_{[[d]]} \)).
distractor_rationales: This restates \( |H| \) instead of the quotient's order.
This restates \( |\mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]}| \) instead of dividing out by \( |H| \).
This is off by one from the correct quotient order.
This is off by one from the correct quotient order.

stem: In \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]} \) (where \( [[n]]=[[d]]\cdot[[m]] \)), let \( H = \langle [[d]] \rangle \), a subgroup of order [[m]], so \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]}/H \cong \mathbb{Z}_{[[d]]} \) via \( x+H \mapsto x \bmod [[d]] \). What is the order of the coset \( [[a]]+H \) in the quotient group \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]}/H \)?
solution: Under the isomorphism \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]}/H \cong \mathbb{Z}_{[[d]]} \), the coset \( [[a]]+H \) corresponds to \( [[a]] \bmod [[d]] \), whose order in \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[d]]} \) is \( [[d]]/\gcd([[d]],[[a]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( \gcd([[d]],[[a]]) \) itself, not \( d \) divided by it.
This restates the representative [[a]] instead of computing its order in the quotient.
This assumes the coset generates the entire quotient group.
This uses \( |H| \), which is unrelated to the coset's order in \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]}/H \).
This is off by one from the correct coset order.
This is off by one from the correct coset order.
